message:
feat(pass2.5): Add inherited method scope tracking and fix variable resolution

Add in_unscoped_method flag to prevent incorrect Pass 2.5 lookups in
inherited methods. When generating methods inherited from a base class,
the derived class doesn't have the method's scope, so Pass 2.5 lookups
could find variables from sibling methods in the base class.

Key changes:
- Add in_unscoped_method field to NativeCodegen
- Set flag based on enterChildScopeOnly() result in function_gen.zig
- Skip Pass 2.5 lookups in getZigName/getZigNameSearchingUp/getDeclZigName
  when in_unscoped_method is true
- Add getZigNameSearchingUp() for ancestor scope lookups
- Add getZigNameAtModuleScope() for module-level class lookups
- Fix various codegen paths to use Pass 2.5 names consistently

Additional fixes:
- Tuple element class references use getZigNameSearchingUp()
- Self-reference detection compares Python names (not Zig names)
- Static captured variables use correct Zig names
- assertIsInstance uses getZigNameSearchingUp() for all non-builtin types
- Class redefinition generates unique names to avoid duplicates
- Nested class constructors use correct scope lookup
- Module-level declarations use Pass 2.5 names
- For-loop hoisted variables use getZigNameSearchingUp()
- Method-local variable shadowing fixed (removed is_module_level check)
- Type inference stores under Python names (not Zig names)

Test status: test_bool 38/38, core1 14/65 (21.5%) - stable
